text: This is the VOA Special English Technology Report.
zh: [0] 这里是美国之音特别英语科技报道。

text: Recently, the Wall Street Journal had a report about the online travel company Orbitz.
zh: [1] 最近，《华尔街日报》报道了在线旅游公司Orbitz。

text: The newspaper said Orbitz shows Mac users higher -priced travel choices
zh: [2] 该报道称，Orbitz向Mac用户展示的搜索结果中，价格较高的旅行选项

text: at the top of search results than users of Windows.
zh: [3] 排在比Windows用户更靠前的位置。

text: The company began doing this after research suggested that people who use Mac computers
zh: [4] 该公司这样做是因为有研究显示，使用Mac电脑的人

text: spend up to 30 % more for hotels than people with PCs.
zh: [5] 在酒店上的花费比使用PC的人多出高达30%。

text: Former Orbitz employee Cameron Yule
zh: [6] Orbitz前员工卡梅伦·尤尔（Cameron Yule）

text: is chief executive of a digital media and technology company called Adgent.
zh: [7] 是一家名为Adgent的数字媒体和技术公司的首席执行官。

text: He says Mac users tend to be more affluent than PC users,
zh: [8] 他说Mac用户往往比PC用户更为富裕，

text: so why not target them with the messages that are going to resonate with them?
zh: [9] 那为什么不用能引起他们共鸣的信息来定向推送呢？

text: This kind of price discrimination is also known as dynamic pricing.
zh: [10] 这种价格歧视也被称为动态定价。

text: Cameron Ewell says it is a very common marketing tool, especially in the travel industry.
zh: [11] 卡梅伦·尤尔说这是一种非常常见的营销手段，尤其是在旅游行业。

text: He puts it this way, next time you're on a flight,
zh: [12] 他这样解释道：下次你坐飞机的时候，

text: ask the person next to you what they paid for their ticket.
zh: [13] 问问坐在你旁边的人，他们的机票花了多少钱。

text: For example, if you are a business traveler
zh: [14] 比如说，如果你是商务旅客，

text: or you bought your ticket at the last minute, you will always get charged more.
zh: [15] 或者你是临时买的票，你总是会被多收费。

text: Companies have always gathered information about their customers.
zh: [16] 公司一直都在收集顾客的信息。

text: But now they have a lot more ways to do it.
zh: [17] 但现在他们有更多方式来做这件事。

text: Eric Martin is a partner at Boost Partners,
zh: [18] 埃里克·马丁（Eric Martin）是Boost Partners的合伙人，

text: a management consulting company. He says a salesperson in a store
zh: [19] 这是一家管理咨询公司。他说商店里的销售人员

text: can only assume certain things about you based on your appearance.
zh: [20] 只能根据你的外表来推测一些关于你的信息。

text: Online sellers, however, have far more information to work with,
zh: [21] 然而，网络卖家可用的信息要多得多，

text: including information about which website directed you to them.
zh: [22] 包括你是通过哪个网站被引导到他们那里的信息。

text: Eric Martin says the people that you deal with online
zh: [23] 埃里克·马丁说，你在网上打交道的人

text: can't see you at all physically, but they know that, for instance,
zh: [24] 完全看不到你本人，但他们知道，比如说，

text: when you came to orbits, you may have been directed there by kayak.
zh: [25] 当你来到Orbitz时，你可能是通过Kayak网站被引导过去的。

text: is itself a comparison site,
zh: [26] 而Kayak本身就是一个比价网站，

text: and that may then lead orbits to believe that you're searching for deals.
zh: [27] 这可能会让Orbitz认为你正在寻找优惠。

text: Information about the referring site is only one of the things
zh: [28] 关于引荐网站的信息只是其中一项，

text: that a site like Orbitz will consider. Is the person a return visitor?
zh: [29] 是像Orbitz这样的网站会考虑的因素之一。这个人是不是回头客？

text: Which hotels has this person viewed in the past? Websites collect this kind of information
zh: [30] 这个人过去浏览过哪些酒店？网站通过在用户电脑上

text: by placing small files known as cookies on on a person's computer.
zh: [31] 放置被称为cookie的小文件来收集这类信息。

text: In the case of Orbitz, Mac and PC users were actually given the same choices.
zh: [32] 就Orbitz而言，Mac用户和PC用户实际上看到的是相同的选项。

text: However, they appeared in different orders.
zh: [33] 只不过它们出现的顺序不同。

text: For Mac users, the higher priced hotels showed up higher in the search results.
zh: [34] 对Mac用户来说，价格较高的酒店在搜索结果中排得更靠前。

text: Orbitz says 90 percent of its users
zh: [35] Orbitz表示，90%的用户

text: book a hotel that appears on the first page and mostly in the top five listings.
zh: [36] 预订的酒店都出现在第一页，且大多在前五名之内。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Mario Ritter.
zh: [37] 这里是美国之音特别英语，我是马里奥·里特（Mario Ritter）。
